title: 10 best herbal capsules for hyperacidity
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hyperacidity are dietary supplements that contain a combination of natural herbs and ingredients, designed to alleviate the symptoms of hyperacidity. These herbs include slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root, which work together to soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and promote the production of mucus in the stomach lining. By doing so, they help protect the stomach from the harmful effects of excessive stomach acid, thereby reducing symptoms such as heartburn, stomach pain, and bloating. For example, a study published in the Journal of Ethnopharmacology found that a herbal supplement containing slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root significantly reduced symptoms of hyperacidity in participants. As a result, herbal capsules for hyperacidity can improve the quality of life for those who suffer from this condition, allowing them to enjoy their daily activities without the discomfort and pain associated with hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-secretory properties that aid in reducing gastric acid production in the stomach. This herbal remedy has been traditionally used to soothe gastrointestinal discomfort and alleviate symptoms associated with acid reflux, such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. Mugwort capsules may also stimulate the production of digestive juices, promoting healthy digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can further help prevent the onset of hyperacidity. Overall, mugwort capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing hyperacidity and maintaining a balanced digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids.

Antioxidant: Mugwort capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent further irritation and inflammation. The antioxidant properties are attributed to compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Prokinetic: Mugwort capsules can stimulate the movement of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help speed up the digestion process and prevent the buildup of stomach acid. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as essential oils and alkaloids.

Carminative: Mugwort capsules can help reduce bloating and gas in the stomach, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as discomfort and pain. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as essential oils and flavonoids.

Astringent: Mugwort capsules can help tighten and tone the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help reduce the production of stomach acid and prevent further irritation. This property is due to the presence of tannins and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried mugwort herb and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the mugwort powder with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch or cellulose as a binding agent.
Gradually add small amounts of water to the mixture until a dough-like consistency is achieved.
Form the dough into small, capsule-shaped pieces using a pill maker or by hand.
Dry the mugwort capsules at room temperature for 24 hours, then store in an airtight container.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Mugwort rhizomes are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and bitter properties that help stimulate digestion and reduce gastric acidity.

Leaves: Mugwort leaves are used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity because they contain essential oils and flavonoids that have been shown to aid in digestion and soothe gastrointestinal irritation.
Flowers: Mugwort flowers are used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity as they contain active compounds that help regulate gastric acid secretion and have a calming effect on the digestive system.

Seeds: Mugwort seeds are included in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity because they are rich in essential oils and antioxidants that support digestion, reduce inflammation, and help regulate stomach acid production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that can help balance stomach acid levels. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux and heartburn. Barberry works by stimulating the production of digestive juices, which aids in proper digestion and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, it helps to strengthen the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing acid reflux, barberry can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Antisecretory: Barberry contains berberine, which helps to reduce gastric acid secretion, thus preventing hyperacidity.
Gastroprotective: The alkaloids in barberry have a cytoprotective effect on the gastric mucosa, protecting it from damage caused by excess acid.
Anti-inflammatory: Barberry's anti-inflammatory properties, due to berberine, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can be a result of hyperacidity.
Spasmolytic: The plant's ability to relax smooth muscles, provided by berberine, can help alleviate spasms in the gastrointestinal tract caused by hyperacidity.
Choleretic: Barberry stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and can help prevent hyperacidity. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids like berberine.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1,000 mg of dried common barberry bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Measure 0.02 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as microcrystalline cellulose, for every 1,000 mg of barberry powder.
Combine the barberry powder and binding agent in a bowl, mixing thoroughly to create a uniform mixture.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed barberry capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for up to six months.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain berberine, which helps balance stomach acid levels and soothes gastrointestinal lining.
Bark: Rich in alkaloids, which help reduce inflammation and provide relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: High in tannins that help protect the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate hyperacidity.
Leaves: Contain berberine and other alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help regulate stomach acid production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and hyperacidity. White tea is also known for its ability to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, including H. pylori, which is often associated with the development of stomach ulcers and acid-related conditions. Additionally, white tea has been shown to help balance stomach acid levels, preventing the production of excessive stomach acid that can cause discomfort and pain in the stomach and chest. Overall, white tea capsules can provide a natural and effective solution for managing hyperacidity and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from hyperacidity. This property is important because hyperacidity can cause inflammation and irritation in the stomach, leading to discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of hyperacidity. This property is important because free radicals can damage the stomach lining, leading to inflammation and hyperacidity. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols and catechins.

Astringent: White tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen the stomach lining, reducing the production of stomach acid. This property is important because hyperacidity is often caused by an overproduction of stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.

Gastroprotective: White tea has gastroprotective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stomach acid, reducing the risk of hyperacidity. This property is important because hyperacidity can cause damage to the stomach lining, leading to discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and caffeine.

Anti-secretory: White tea has anti-secretory properties that can help reduce the production of stomach acid, providing relief from hyperacidity. This property is important because hyperacidity is often caused by an overproduction of stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather and crush 2 teaspoons of dried bitter melon, 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root, and 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered herbs with 1 cup of boiling water, let it steep for 15 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the cooled herbal mixture with 1 teaspoon of cornstarch, forming a paste.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal paste, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Let the capsules dry for 24 hours before storing them in an airtight container in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to help reduce stomach acid and inflammation, making them ideal for managing hyperacidity.
Buds: The unopened buds of the white tea plant contain high levels of polyphenols, which can help regulate stomach acid production and soothe gastrointestinal discomfort.
Rhyzomes: White tea rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and indigestion.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains tannins, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production, providing relief from hyperacidity symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains caraway oil, which has been traditionally used in treating digestive issues. The oil's key component, carvone, has been found to stimulate the production of gastric juices, thereby aiding in digestion and reducing acidity in the stomach. Additionally, caraway oil has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hyperacidity. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing acid production, caraway capsules can help manage hyperacidity and provide relief from its symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-secretory: Caraway capsules have the ability to reduce gastric acid secretion, which is important for hyperacidity as it helps to prevent the excess production of stomach acid. This property is attributed to the active constituents, particularly the alkaloids and flavonoids present in caraway.

Anti-spasmodic: Caraway capsules help in reducing smooth muscle spasms, which is important for hyperacidity as it alleviates the pain and discomfort caused by stomach cramps and spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property include the volatile oil and flavonoids found in caraway.

Carminative: Caraway capsules have the ability to expel gas from the stomach, which is important for hyperacidity as it helps to relieve symptoms such as bloating, flatulence, and abdominal pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include the volatile oil, particularly carvone and limonene, present in caraway.

Gastroprotective: Caraway capsules help in protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acid production, which is important for hyperacidity as it helps to prevent gastric ulcers and other complications. The active constituents responsible for this property include the flavonoids, tannins, and saponins present in caraway.

Choleretic: Caraway capsules help in increasing the production of bile, which is important for hyperacidity as it promotes digestion and prevents the formation of excess stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property include the volatile oil and flavonoids present in caraway.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried caraway seeds and grind them into a fine powder, which should take approximately 5 minutes.
Mix the caraway seed powder with 1 teaspoon of baking soda and 1 cup of water, stirring until fully combined.
Allow the mixture to sit for 15 minutes to activate the baking soda.
Form the mixture into small, equal-sized capsules using a capsule maker, making sure each capsule contains an equal amount of the mixture.
Let the capsules dry for 24 hours before storing them in a cool, dry place.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for hyperacidity due to their carminative properties that help reduce gas and bloating.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of caraway plant are used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity as they contain volatile oils that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes.
Leaves: Caraway leaves are used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity because they contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peruvian bark capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains high alkaloid content, particularly the compound called cinchonine, which is known to have anti-inflammatory and acid-neutralizing properties. These alkaloids work by coating the stomach lining, protecting it from excessive acid production, and reducing inflammation. As a result, the capsule helps alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, stomach pain, and indigestion. Additionally, it promotes the production of digestive enzymes, improving overall digestion and nutrient absorption. Its natural healing properties make it a safer and more gentle alternative to conventional hyperacidity medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peruvian bark (Cinchona officinalis) contains alkaloids such as quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating hyperacidity.
Antacid: Peruvian bark has antacid properties due to its alkaloid content, which can neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Mucoprotective: The bark's alkaloids and tannins form a protective layer on the gastric mucosa, reducing exposure to acid and promoting healing of damaged tissue caused by hyperacidity.
Astringent: Peruvian bark contains tannins that have astringent properties, which can help reduce gastric secretions, providing symptomatic relief from hyperacidity.

Prokinetic: Certain alkaloids in Peruvian bark, like quinine, can stimulate gastric motility, helping to empty the stomach more quickly and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux, a common symptom of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Peruvian bark, 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root, and 1 teaspoon of dried marshmallow root.
Grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herbs with enough gelatin capsule filler to make 5-10 capsules.
Fill size "00" gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily as needed for hyperacidity relief.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Peruvian bark plant contains alkaloids that can help reduce stomach acid and heal stomach ulcers, making it effective for treating hyperacidity.
Roots: The roots of the plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation in the stomach, providing relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain tannins that can help reduce stomach acid and protect the stomach lining, making them useful for making medicinal capsules for hyperacidity.
Seeds: The seeds of the Peruvian bark plant contain essential oils and alkaloids that can help regulate stomach acid production, providing relief from hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coptis chinensis capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains berberine, a powerful alkaloid that can help balance stomach acid levels. This herb has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat digestive issues, including acid reflux and hyperacidity. The anti-inflammatory properties of Coptis chinensis can also help soothe the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort. Furthermore, it has been shown to inhibit the growth of H. pylori, a bacterium that can contribute to hyperacidity and other digestive problems. By addressing the root causes of hyperacidity, Coptis chinensis capsule can provide effective and natural relief for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Coptis chinensis contains alkaloids such as berberine, which helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating hyperacidity.
Antimicrobial: The presence of berberine and other alkaloids in Coptis chinensis exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat hyperacidity caused by H. pylori infections.
Astringent: Coptis chinensis has tannins that exhibit astringent properties, helping to protect the mucous membrane of the stomach, reducing hyperacidity.
Antioxidant: The high flavonoid content in Coptis chinensis provides antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing hyperacidity.
Hypochlorhydric: Coptis chinensis can decrease gastric secretions and reduce stomach acid production, making it beneficial for treating hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried Coptis chinensis root into a fine powder, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.
Mix the powder with 1 tablespoon of a binding agent, such as magnesium stearate or microcrystalline cellulose.
Measure out 0.5 gram portions of the powder mixture and fill size "0" gelatin capsules, allowing 20-30 minutes for completion.
Allow the filled capsules to dry completely for 1-2 hours before storing in a tightly sealed container.
Take 1-2 capsules, as needed, 30 minutes before meals to help manage hyperacidity symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of alkaloids, which can help reduce gastric acid production and provide relief from hyperacidity.
Rhizomes: Rich in berberine and other isoquinoline alkaloids, which can inhibit gastric acid secretion and help manage hyperacidity symptoms.
Leaves: Contain various beneficial compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, that may help regulate stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
Seeds: Packed with active constituents, such as palmatine and jatrorrhizine, which can help balance stomach acidity and promote digestive health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. Curcumin helps to balance stomach acid levels, reducing the symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, bloating, and stomach pain. Additionally, it protects the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acid production, promoting overall digestive health. Furthermore, turmeric's calming properties help to reduce stress-induced hyperacidity, making it a holistic approach to managing this common digestive issue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is attributed to curcumin, the active compound in turmeric.

Mucosa protective: Turmeric has the ability to protect the gastric mucosa, which is the inner lining of the stomach, from damage caused by hyperacidity. This is important because it can help prevent the worsening of symptoms and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are curcumin and other curcuminoids.

Antioxidant: Turmeric has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from free radical damage. This is important for hyperacidity as it can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Curcumin is the primary active constituent responsible for turmeric's antioxidant properties.

Antisecretory: Turmeric has the ability to reduce gastric acid secretion, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation. The active constituents responsible for this property are curcumin and other curcuminoids.

Prokinetic: Turmeric can help stimulate the movement of food through the digestive tract, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent the buildup of stomach acid. The active constituents responsible for this property are curcumin and other curcuminoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather and measure ingredients: 1 cup turmeric root, 1/2 cup ginger root, 1/4 cup fennel seeds, and 1/4 cup dried licorice root.
Clean and chop turmeric and ginger roots into small pieces.
Combine all ingredients in a blender or spice grinder, blend until a fine powder forms (approximately 5-10 minutes).
Fill size "00" empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the herbal powder using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dry place, in an airtight container, for up to 6 months.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for hyperacidity due to their high curcumin content, which helps balance stomach acid levels.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant have a similar medicinal profile to the rhizomes and are used interchangeably in capsule production for treating hyperacidity.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves contain beneficial compounds, but they are less commonly used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity, as the rhizomes and roots are more potent sources of active ingredients.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers have minimal medicinal value compared to other plant parts, and they are rarely, if ever, included in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are not typically used in medicinal capsules for hyperacidity, as they contain lower levels of active ingredients compared to the rhizomes and roots.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains diosgenin, a compound that can be converted into various steroidal hormones, including cortisone, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA), and progesterone. These hormones can help regulate the function of the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, and promoting the production of digestive enzymes and juices. This can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, bloating, and stomach pain. Additionally, wild yam has anti-spasmodic properties that can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort. Overall, wild yam capsule can provide a natural and effective way to manage hyperacidity and promote digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild yam capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort caused by excessive stomach acid. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the presence of steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin.

Mucilaginous: Wild yam has a mucilaginous property that can form a protective coating on the stomach lining, which is crucial for hyperacidity as it can help prevent the stomach lining from being damaged by excessive acid. This property is due to the high mucilage content in wild yam.

Antispasmodic: Wild yam capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for hyperacidity as it can reduce spasms and cramping caused by acid reflux. The antispasmodic properties are attributed to the presence of steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin.

Astringent: Wild yam capsules can help tighten and tone the tissues of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help reduce the production of stomach acid. The astringent properties are attributed to the presence of tannins in wild yam.

Demulcent: Wild yam capsules can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. The demulcent properties are attributed to the high mucilage content in wild yam.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of wild yam root and carefully clean and chop into small pieces.
Combine chopped wild yam root with 4 cups of water in a pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid, keeping the liquid and discarding the solid remains.
Allow the liquid to cool, then mix in 0.5 cup of a natural binder such as tapioca starch.
Form the mixture into small capsules and let them dry for 24 hours before storing in a cool, dry place.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild yam contain diosgenin, which can be converted to progesterone and other steroids that can help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
Rhizomes: Wild yam rhizomes are rich in saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, making them useful for treating digestive issues such as hyperacidity.
Leaves: The leaves of wild yam contain flavonoids and tannins, which have astringent properties that can help protect and soothe the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms of hyperacidity.

Seeds: Wild yam seeds contain essential oils and alkaloids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, making them useful for treating digestive issues such as hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with hyperacidity because it contains active compounds that can increase the mucus lining of the stomach, providing a protective barrier against acid. This herbal remedy has been shown to help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, thereby alleviating the symptoms of hyperacidity. Additionally, licorice can help improve digestion and regulate stomach acid production, making it an effective natural treatment for hyperacidity. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of licorice make it a valuable tool in managing this common digestive complaint.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Mucosal protective: Licorice forms a protective mucus layer on the stomach lining, which helps prevent the erosion caused by stomach acid. This property is attributed to the glycyrrhizin and polysaccharides found in licorice.
Antacid: Licorice has antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from symptoms of hyperacidity. The flavonoids in licorice contribute to this antacid effect.

Gastric mucosal cytoprotection: Licorice aids in the regeneration of stomach lining cells, promoting faster healing of damaged tissues. The compound glabridin in licorice is responsible for this cytoprotective effect.

Anti-ulcer: Licorice has been shown to have anti-ulcer properties, which can help prevent and heal ulcers caused by hyperacidity. The active constituents responsible for this property include glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and triterpenoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder, which may take 20-30 minutes.
Measure 500 mg of the licorice root powder and mix it with an appropriate binder such as magnesium stearate.
Fill size "00" empty capsules with the licorice root-binder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers, preferably in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight.
Consult with a healthcare professional for appropriate dosage and potential drug interactions before consuming.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help increase the mucus lining of the stomach, protecting it from hyperacidity.
Seeds: Licorice seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat gastric ulcers and hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, making it useful for managing hyperacidity.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are rich in triterpenoids and saponins, which can help reduce gastric inflammation and provide relief from symptoms of hyperacidity.

Flowers: Licorice flowers contain various antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production, making them useful for managing hyperacidity.

These compounds can help balance stomach acidity, reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, and soothe gastrointestinal discomfort. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roselle capsules can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort caused by excess stomach acid. remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried roselle calyces and grind them into a fine powder, taking approximately 10 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperacidity with herbal capsules is by using products standardized for active ingredients known to reduce symptoms. Slippery elm, licorice, and marshmallow root can help protect and soothe the stomach lining, while deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) reduces inflammation and acid production. Additionally, look for herbs with anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, such as chamomile, ginger, and turmeric, to alleviate discomfort and promote healthy digestion. Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and recommendations tailored to your needs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal capsules include acid reflux, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), and functional dyspepsia. Herbal remedies, such as capsules containing slippery elm, marshmallow root, or chamomile, can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, reduce acid production, and improve overall digestion. These natural alternatives often have fewer side effects compared to conventional medications and can be effective for long-term use. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen to ensure safety and efficacy.
